1000→1. Communication is an art to be mastered by all leaders  
  1001→2. Proper communication can arrest rumours and grape vine  
  1002→3. Communication provides clarity to employees and eradicates confusion  
  1003→4. Communication is a medium to share good ideas across organisation
  1004→
  1005→**CHAPTER : THE “MISUSE” SYNDROME**
  1006→
  1007→Some Leaders are habitually unethical and irresponsible spenders of money of the company. 
  1008→
  1009→Once a new ED was recruited in an organisation. Several EDs and Directors in the past worked successfully in the existing office but this ED thought that his office needs a grand makeover. The building also housed hundreds of employees of different levels. Upon proposal and convincing the Board, a hefty sum was approved and a complete renovation was carried out. The exchequer was quite high. After a few months, the President thought that the company’s Head Office should have been in another big city, incidentally where his home town was located. This too was approved by the Board and a new office was taken where all the top leaders of the company were relocated. The earlier President and the newly renovated ED’s office remained vacant as a waste while the other employees continued working in the same building in other floors as they were earlier. 
  1010→
  1011→Several questions come to one’s mind. Was such renovation of the ED’s existing office required? Was relocation of Top Management Office to a swanky office elsewhere required? Why was not this relocation of office thought before so that the renovation of ED’s office could have been avoided? Why, after a short time after renovation did thought come across to relocate to a bigger city? Are these expenses worthwhile with respect to the financials of the company? Did the earlier ED or Directors face so much of problems of space or inconvenience that they could not work properly? Could such renovation or part relocation wait for some more time? Can the relocated top leaders have meaningful coordination with the existing office now where most of their employees are still housed? Did not the Board think of all these issues while they provided their approval? Isn’t it therefore that such a renovation or relocation was a waste of company’s money?
  1012→
  1013→In another instance, customer’s representative comes to your office at around 11 am for a meeting. The leader craves for a good lunch. It’s quiet sometime that he did not savour a good buffet lunch in a five star hotel\! So, what does the Leader do? He unnecessarily hosts a lunch for the guest in a five star hotel after the meeting just to satisfy himself for the lunch even if the guests were not keen for the lunch. Very cunningly, the leader takes along with him a close subordinate for the lunch so that the subordinate signs off the bill (the leader being not the spender\!) who then sends the bill to the same leader for approval. The leader signs the bill. Alas\! Rs 25,000 gone from the coffers of the company simply for the fact that the leader actually wanted a good lunch, not the guests\!  
  1014→
  1015→Sometimes, to maintain cordial relationship, hosting a lunch or a dinner for a stakeholder is nothing wrong, but to do it numerous times to satisfy your own greed for good food at the cost of the company is unethical and waste of company’s money.
  1016→
  1017→Accounts Department needs to be vigilant on such avoidable expenses which are actually personal in nature but made official like I mentioned above. 
  1018→
  1019→Once there was a marriage of a Director’s daughter of a big client. The Director extended invitation to the Leader personally and not to others in the company. Hence this was purely a personal invitation. However, the Leader convinces his Management and Accounts that the invitation is to the company and therefore a gift needs to be presented on behalf of the company. The Management, upon trust, assumes that the invitation has been extended to some top leaders of the SBU and thereby approves the cost for an expensive gift. However, when the gift is handed over, it is done in a manner as if the gift has been presented by the leader in his personal capacity. 
  1020→
  1021→In all such cases where actually the leader was supposed to spend money ethically from his own pocket, he disguises every time as if the incidents are related to the company and thereafter spends the money from the coffers of the company. A leader is supposedly paid well; still he hankers after company’s money for personal expenses. It is an irony that such well to do people in a company are hungry to grab more and more facilities unethically and make or save more money irrespective of the wealth they may have already amassed. 
  1022→
  1023→Whether you want to make or save money, that’s entirely up to you, but definitely you cannot trick a company to pay for your personal events from their coffers\!  
  1024→
  1025→**The salient points are:**
  1026→
  1027→1. Expenses of a company must be optimized and regulated  
  1028→2. Leaders must refrain from spending company’s expenses for their own vested interests  
  1029→3. HR and Accounts must be vigilant to stop unnecessary spending from the company’s coffers by dishonest leaders
  1030→
  1031→**CHAPTER : THE “BEST PROJECT AWARD” SYNDROME**
  1032→
  1033→The best executed project at site is the most deserving project for an award for a leader, but unfortunately the leader makes mistakes in such determination. Site execution cannot be the only criteria to be judged as the best executed project without taking cognisance of how and at what costs the project was financially closed. Therefore a Project should wait for its financial closure to be determined for award because rework during warranty period might reduce the profitability as determined after the physical completion of the project. Projects competing for awards must be financially closed first. Projects physically completed and commissioned but not financially closed should not be considered for award.
  1034→
  1035→Many management students in B-schools are not aware of what are all the terms of a financial closure of a project. Briefly, the same are as follows.
  1036→
  1037→1\. Physical completion of the project  
  1038→2\. Commissioning of the project, i.e. say for an electric transmission project, the successful commencement of transmission of electricity  
  1039→3\. Complete reconciliation of all billings billed to the client for the project and the payments received by the contractor  
  1040→4\. Providing Retention Bank Guarantee  
  1041→5\. Replacements and rework, if necessary, during warranty period at contractor’s cost  
  1042→6\. Successful completion of warranty period.  
  1043→7\. Retrieval of all bank guarantees from the client.
  1044→
  1045→As illustrated in a later chapter on the Project Health Card, the project parameters to be considered for the best project award are as follows:
  1046→
  1047→1\. Construction Time  
  1048→2\. Direct & Indirect costs, overheads, finance costs, Total costs w.r.t. budget  
  1049→3\. Profitability w.r.t Construction Time after considering rework and replacements during warranty period  
  1050→4\. Use of Technology  
  1051→5\. Quality of construction  
  1052→6\. Safety methods implemented during construction  
  1053→7\. Ethical standards  
  1054→8\. Sustainability implementation  
  1055→9\. CSR project undertaken   
  1056→10\. Cash Flow of the project  
  1057→11\. Financial closure of project
  1058→
  1059→The SBU Head should provide a weightage to each of the above parameters for each project closed financially and thereafter judge which project is the best FINANCIALLY closed project. Normally the leader tend to judge the best project based on the top three parameters as illustrated above, which is wrong.
  1060→
  1061→Use of Technology ensures better quality of work. Quality of construction ensures longer durability of the project. Safety methods ensure safety and wellbeing of human lives. Ethical standards raise the name and fame of an organisation. Sustainability measures indicate caring for your environment and the future of our earth. Cash Flow determines the requirement of capital for the project. And financial closure frees the organisation from all financial obligations of the project. Hence the other eight parameters too deserve significant weightage.
  1062→
  1063→Suppose a project calls for huge cash capital for execution of the project but yield average profits. The capital requirement of the project may be so big that it may have disturbed the cash flow and timely completion of other projects. Then the question is whether such an order was worth for the company? Can such a financially closed project involving huge capital and yielding average profits be considered for award even if it was executed well? Thus determining the best project for award is a critical judgment based on all the parameters discussed and their associated weightages.
  1064→
  1065→Often the leaders consider a particular project as the best without deep diving on all the parameters of a good executed and financially closed project. Best executed project is also sometimes chosen based on the leader’s relationship with the Project Manager. Such malpractices will dissolve if there is a fair mechanism to judge the best project.
  1066→
  1067→**The salient points are:**
  1068→
  1069→1. The best financially closed project is the best executed project  
  1070→2. Various parameters of Financial closure must be taken into account for evaluating the best project   
  1071→3. Award must not be given to best physically completed project but given to best financially closed project which also considers physical completion
  1072→
  1073→**CHAPTER : THE “BID LOSS BLAME” SYNDROME**
  1074→
  1075→In a bid, various components of costs are considered by the Bidding team of an organisation. The Design Head provides design data based on which costs are evaluated. The Supply Chain Head provides best quotes of vendors. The Finance Head provides the best financing and bank guarantee costs. The Project Management Head provides the best Site management and overhead costs. The HR Head provides the salaries of different grades of Project and Site manpower. The Advertisement Head provides the annual advertising costs to be appropriated and considered in each bid. The Equipment Management Head provides the cost of using equipment and machinery for a project, and so on and so forth.
  1076→
  1077→The Bidding Head is responsible to collate all information, documents and costs from various departments and compile the price bid to be submitted to the client. So far as costs are considered, he discusses and negotiates with all departmental Heads to get the best competitive costs to be incorporated in the bid. Of course the Bidding Head also considers the market, competition, likely quote of competitors, importance of winning the project to determine the profit levels to be considered before finalizing the price of the bid.
  1078→
  1079→Some costs mentioned above have a major impact in the bid cost, and some costs have low impact. For example, the vendor costs have a major impact in the bid cost since supplies may take up 70% of the total bid cost.
  1080→
  1081→People should understand that Bidding & Marketing department provides only the Marketing costs of the company to be incorporated as overhead costs in the bid. All other costs are from other different Departmental Heads. The main job of the Bidding team and the Head is to put a proper documentation of all techno-commercial requirements as required and specified in the bidding documents (Part – 1: Techno-commercial bid) and the Price Schedules of different Supplies and Services required for the Project (Part – 2: Price bid)
  1082→
  1083→Now here is the problem. Whenever a bid is lost, the Director often pounces on the Bidding Head as if he was the main culprit for losing the bid. All other Departmental Heads like the Finance Head, HR Head, SCM Head, etc. are scot free and takes no accountability for the loss even if the quotes given by them during bid submission were actually not competitive. It is shameful that the Bidding Head is often the scape goat and the poor chap has to bear the brunt of bid losses. Such a thought process needs to change in the industry. In fact, I will discuss in more details in a subsequent chapter, that one of the evaluation criteria in the Annual Appraisals **of all Departments** of an organisation that must be incorporated is the percentage of bid win. The weightage of such criteria across different Departments may vary depending on the cost impact of the Departmental cost towards bid cost. Unfortunately, the weightage for percentage of bids won in all other Departments is not considered as a KRA in their Appraisals, as if no other Department other than the Marketing & Bidding Department was responsible for bid losses. This is a very unfortunate phenomenon existing in organisations. Various Departments must be responsible for bid losses to the extent of their cost impact towards the bid. Similarly, they must be rewarded for bid wins to the extent of their cost impacts. It is also not correct that the bidding team takes full credit of bid wins when other departments contributed to the bid cost leading to winning bids.
  1084→
  1085→Particularly, the major costs of a bid are the vendors which may take up 65% to 80% of total bid cost and the Project management costs at site which may take up 20% to 30% of the bid cost. Hence the SCM Head responsible for vendor costs and the PM Head responsible for Project Management costs are the major cost impact departments. Other departments may impact around 10% of bid costs which cannot be ignored because even a non-competitive cost offer from this 10% impact zone may lead to a bid loss by a thin margin. 
  1086→
  1087→Also there is a question of a bad win and a good win. This aspect will be discussed in a separate chapter.
  1088→
  1089→Therefore, when a bid is lost, a Director may question not only the Bidding Head but also the SCM Head and the PM or Operations Head. The Bidding Head cannot be the only scape goat. 
  1090→
  1091→**The salient points are:**
  1092→
  1093→1. A Price bid has to consider costs given by different Departments  
  1094→2. The SCM and the Project Management Departments are the bulk of the costs in a Price bid  
  1095→3. The Bidding Department develops only the Marketing Costs on their own, all other costs are provided by other Departments  
  1096→4. The Bidding Head alone cannot be responsible for bid wins and losses  
  1097→5. Appraisals must provide appropriate weightage to all Departments for bid wins and losses based on their cost impact in the Price bid 
  1098→
  1099→**CHAPTER : THE “WILD DREAMS” SYNDROME**

content.text: Let me sample from the middle section of the file:

content.content:
2000→
  2001→**CHAPTER : HAVING LION’S EYE, NOT AN EAGLE’S**
  2002→
  2003→Do you know how many hours does a lion sleep? It’s around 18 to 20 hours. The balance time in a day is for hunting an eating. So a lion is basically a very lazy animal.
  2004→
  2005→Also do you know the power of the eyesight of an eagle? Its expertise in picking up its moving target on ground is highly impressive, just like a kingfisher picks up a fish from a river within seconds.
  2006→
  2007→Why am I talking about all these animal instincts? I will tell you why in just a while.
  2008→
  2009→A tender comprises of techno commercial and financial specifications, may run into thousands of pages which a bidder has to carefully go through and prepare his bid. Ignoring any important line or clause in the specifications may cost the organisation dearly. There are a multiple examples of this. I will cite some.
  2010→
  2011→The first example is that of mine. When I was the BD Manager, a tender for construction of a particular Transmission Line was announced. I prepared a good techno commercial and price bid and submitted the same to the client. Later on I was shocked to know that our bid was rejected. One of the lines amongst thousands of pages of specifications read that the bidder must be a manufacturer of tower structures, which we were not at that point of time. I somehow missed the line while I read the voluminous bidding documents. And this cost the organisation dearly because had our bid opened we would have been L1 and secured the order.  
  2012→   
  2013→In another example, there were two tenders related to the same project, the first tender for the first geographical portion of the project and the second one for the next geographical portion of the same project, say Tender 1 and Tender 2\. To maintain secrecy of the final bid price,   
  2014→the Head asked the junior engineers to prepare bid prices with escalated rates for all items, the intention being that he will submit a discount vide a discount letter and attach with the bid to arrive at the final bid price. The Head wanted to attach a 5% discount letter for Tender 1 and a 15% discount letter for Tender 2 to arrive at his intended final bid prices. The Head then did a massive blunder. He put the 15% discount letter in Tender 1 and the 5% discount letter in Tender 2\. So what happened is that the Tender 1 submitted became undervalued (ie. would incur loss when executed) and Tender 2 became overvalued. The result was that the organisation won the contract against Tender 1 and lost lot of money after it was executed. And Tender 2 being overvalued was lost. Remember, after a bid is submitted or awarded, you cannot rectify your mistakes. That is the rule of bids and awards.
  2015→
  2016→Another example was very interesting. The specifications said that the bids must be submitted on so and so date by 9.30 am. The bid had to be submitted on the third floor of the client’s office building. Many bidders submitted their bid by 9.30 am; however it was astonishing to find that a very competitive bidder had not submitted its bid by that time. Many bidders were happy at the thought that the competition would be less. However, all of a sudden from nowhere, this competitive bidder came running and submitted his bid at 9.35 am. All other bidders represented that this late bid should not be accepted by the client. However, this bidder represented that he had entered the client’s office and registered his attendance at 9.28 am but then he waited for the lift in the building and it was 9.35 am by the time he could submit the bid. Amidst this confusion, the client immediately instituted a committee of directors and finally the committee directed that the bid be accepted since the bidder had registered his attendance in the office of reception of the client at 9.28 am, thereby his bid will not be considered as late. 
  2017→
  2018→The English of the specifications can sometimes be confusing leading to rejection of bids. For example, one specification stated “the contractor must have experience in three similar transmission line projects of minimum 100 kms which involved supply of conductors and insulators in the project”. Now there can be different interpretations like this:
  2019→
  2020→1. Do all the three projects required supply of conductors and insulators both?  
  2021→2. Does it mean that one project could have supplied insulators only and the other two supplied conductors?  
  2022→3. What happens if one of transmission line had no supply of conductors or insulators but the other two had?  
  2023→4. Is it that each of the transmission line should have been 100 kms long?  
  2024→5. Is it that the cumulative length of all the transmission lines should be atleast 100 kms long?
  2025→
  2026→Sometimes the English used in specifications can be distorted and differently interpreted by different bidders. In such cases, it is best to raise clarifications to the client in writing and act accordingly. 
  2027→
  2028→Numerous such interesting examples can be given. But in essence what I am trying to drive at is that the Bidding & Costing Department must keep their eyes open and has to be as sharp as that of an eagle’s. You cannot be lazy like a lion and miss the details of a bid. In all cases of blunders cited above, they were cases of oversight. Heads and employees of Bidding & Costing cannot be excused for such oversights because you lose lot of opportunities for the organisation and at times lose lot of money for such oversights.
  2029→
  2030→Because of my oversight in the first case, I was reprimanded. However, it was a big learning for me to be extremely careful thereafter and always read specifications with an eagle’s eye. I never used to read specifications if I was stressed or not feeling like to read at any point of time.
  2031→
  2032→HR must ensure that Heads and managers in Bidding & Costing are very meticulous in reading specifications without oversight. You must be an eagle, not a lion.
  2033→
  2034→**The salient points of concern are:**
  2035→
  2036→1. A bidding engineer must be meticulous in studying the bid documents  
  2037→2. Submission of documents for different tenders must be carefully packaged so as to not mix documents amongst tenders  
  2038→3. If a clause arise multiple interpretations, ask clarifications in writing to the client
  2039→
  2040→**CHAPTER : BEING THE RODENT, NOT THE OWL**
  2041→
  2042→The rodent is known as one of the most impatient animal in the world. Every second it wants to move in search of food. On the contrary the owl is the most rare patient spirit animal and creature. In case you encounter an owl, the universe is giving you a sign. This animal represents wisdom, patience, solitude, deep connection and intuitive knowledge.  
  2043→   
  2044→In the department of Bidding & Costing, one needs prolonged concentration and patience to go through the huge bidding documents minutely keeping a note of all the important clauses and terms of the specifications based on which the techno-commercial bid and the price bid would be prepared.
  2045→
  2046→There are men who are often distracted during work for different reasons including time off for smoking. There are women who are also often distracted for various reasons including time off for facial touch ups. I am not discouraging either of them, but what I am trying to mean is that people in this department needs to have deep concentration for prolonged periods before breaks so that they do not miss the minute details of the documents. It has been observed that employees resorting to regular breaks often miss some important points of the documents, particularly at the point they left reading before the break and commence reading after the break.   
  2047→   
  2048→Once, someone took a break while being on the middle of a chapter while going through a technical document. After the break, he resumed browsing the specifications, but missed a paragraph while doing so. The paragraph contained an important product parameter to be considered in the bid, which was ignored during the preparation of the bid. It’s a simple guess for what happened after the submission of the bid. The bid was technically rejected.
  2049→
  2050→In another instance, I was anxious about someone’s health while I was reading a specification requirement that the bidder has to be a manufacturer for a particular product. I did not understand its importance probably due to lack of concentration and I did not make this a point to be considered in the bid. Eventually the bid was rejected.  
  2051→
  2052→So specifications have to be read minutely in order not to miss any points in any chapter. It helps to make your technical and commercial bid responsive. HR and top leaders must ensure that other than the requisites of an employee to work in the Bidding department, prolonged patience to go through huge bidding documents is a must. 
  2053→
  2054→You must display the characteristics of an owl being patient and concentrating on every move, not the rodent being distracted here and there every moment.  
  2055→
  2056→**The salient points of concern are:**
  2057→
  2058→1. A bidding engineer must have ample patience to read voluminous bidding documents.  
  2059→2. A bidding engineer must love reading bidding documents. If you do not like reading after a while, you are bound to miss some important points.  
  2060→3. Many engineers do not have the power of concentration. They will not be a good fit in the bidding department. This is not to say that they are bad, they may be fit well in some other departments.  
  2061→
  2062→**CHAPTER : DIVULGENCE FOR GAINS**
  2063→
  2064→Bidding and Costing is a sensitive department. Every detail needs to be kept secret because you are competing with the bids of others. So any information leaked from your bid to your competitor may prove to be very dangerous leading to loss of bids.
  2065→
  2066→There are some scrupulous people who take advantage of this. They pass on secret information of bid and costs to other competitors in lieu of unethical money. These people are dishonest and are not loyal to their own company. They know that they can earn good money by such unethical means though their company may not be able to win tenders. If situation comes that the company is struggling for existence due to continuous loss of bids, they would switch over to some other companies and resort to malpractices there also.
  2067→
  2068→Bidding team members must keep a thorough and a minute watch if such malpractice is adopted by any team member. HR and the Vigilance team must also keep a close watch. Bid opening results for certain bids may also provide a clue if something of this sort is happening in the department. The Marketing Head should monitor which bid has been prepared by which engineer leading to bid losses despite preparing competitive bids. If required, employees may be interrogated for foul play.
  2069→
  2070→It may so happen that other department employees roam freely in the bidding department and get hold of certain crucial data which can be passed on to a competitor for vested interest. Therefore this department must have a separate glass enclosure within the office premises with permission to access by other department employees if need be.
  2071→
  2072→**The salient points of concern are:**
  2073→
  2074→1. The Marketing Head, HR, Vigilance team and bidding team members must be vigilant if any data is being leaked outside by any employee for personal gains.  
  2075→2. The bidding area must have a separate enclosure with locked doors making it inaccessible to any other department. Permission to access the bidding department may be on need basis.
  2076→
  2077→**CHAPTER : MALPRACTICE OF MISCOSTING**
  2078→
  2079→Winning a bid against competitors is a tough job. A decent strike rate for winning tenders could be between 10% and 15% where there are a number of competitors in the fray.
  2080→
  2081→Sometimes a incapable bidding engineer, though might have been recruited based on interviews earlier, fails to prepare competitive bids. So they resort to malpractice as follows.
  2082→
  2083→In a complicated bid like that for an EPC project, there are thousands of cost data to be analysed and derived. For example, for a simple item like concreting a floor slab of a building, you need to know the costs of cement, sand, coarse aggregates, reinforcement of different sizes, wires to bind the reinforcement, transportation costs of all these materials from their sources to the nearest point for construction, head-loading cost of these materials from the nearest road point to the actual site, cost of shuttering, placement of reinforcement, mixing concrete, usage of equipment like mixer, vibrator, labour costs, etc.
  2084→
  2085→Similarly, there are hundreds of items in a complicated project where thousands of data has to be analysed. It is not possible for a Head to leave all his work and check all these data analysed by the bidding engineer. Taking advantage of this, a unethical engineer, in order to try to win a bid, may intentionally consider lesser cost of materials than it is so in reality, and make the bid competitive, however rendering it un-executable financially. The bidding engineer may be further cunning enough to manipulate these costs of items which would actually be executed at the end of the project execution. For example, the electrical and sanitary fittings are executed at the end of the project execution.
  2086→
  2087→The advantage is that the bidding engineer gets sufficient time to plan his course of action before getting caught. Bid evaluation of a project may take 4 to 6 months, another month for award, designs and engineering may take 4 to 6 months of time, and thereafter the item of construction where the bidding engineer may have manipulated would come up after another year. So it’s almost two years that the manipulated item of construction is taken up. Therefore the engineer gets two years time before the company understands that the items have been under quoted. Two years is a long time by which the engineer would have mal-practiced by under quoting in many bids. Many such bids would have been awarded and during this time of two years the engineer may have been congratulated, facilitated, awarded and promoted with good bonuses\! Then when the time comes after two years before he is exposed, he switches over to other companies in case he is accused for such under quoting or carries on in the company if no punishment is given to him.
  2088→
  2089→The Head, by virtue of his position, must be aware of such malpractices. He has estimates of different costs because of his experience. So he should look into the final costs, instead of going into the details for which time would not be available) that engineer has prepared before finalising the bid. If the Head gives time to overview the costing before a bid is submitted, he can easily detect if under quoting has been resorted to.
  2090→
  2091→Also after a bid is won, the Project manager assigned for the construction of the project after award may go through all the item costs right from the beginning to end to understand if the costing of all the items have been done rightly.
  2092→
  2093→**The salient points of concern are:**
  2094→
  2095→1. Some bidding engineer may resort to under quote some items of the bid intentionally to be lower price and win bids.  
  2096→2. Such bids when awarded are actually un-executable financially.  
  2097→3. Marketing Head must browse all item costs with his experience before the bid is submitted to understand if any malpractice has been adopted.  
  2098→4. The assigned Project Manager, on bid becoming L1, can validate cost of all items. However, any malpractice of under quote found at this stage will put the bidder in financial crisis since the bidder cannot change prices at this point of time.  
  2099→
  2100→**CHAPTER : THE EXTRA TIME AND VALIDTY PROBLEM**
  2101→
  2102→Sometimes upon request of the bidder in order to complete a bid preparation or because of the requirement of a client, the bid submission time is extended. The bidder in the meantime may have received offers from vendors which are valid for the required period of time as per the original specification. For example, say a bid was supposed to be submitted on 1st January of any year with a bid validity of 3 months, ie. Valid till the end of March 31st; but the time of bid submission is extended to 15th January by the client with the same bid terms, then the different mistakes that the bidder often overlooks are the following.
  2103→
  2104→1. The vendors and subcontractors may have submitted their offers with validity till 31st March. But with the extension of time of bid submission by 15 days; the offers required validity till 15th April in line with client’s terms. But the same might have been ignored by the bidder. Now if the award is given by the client say on 10th April, then the offers of the vendors at this time of award is not valid. The vendor can rightly say that their offer is not valid if it finds that the offer terms and prices are not appropriate during this period of time due to cost escalations. The vendor has every right to change its prices and terms after its original validity and therefore the bidder is in deep trouble.
  2105→
  2106→2. Another mistake that a bidder does is that, even if the time for bid submission is not extended by the client, the bidder does not ask for a validity of offer from their vendors and subcontractors for at least one month more beyond the validity of the bid. This practice should be resorted to because if the client places the order on 31st March (ie. Within bid validity period), thereafter the SCM department may take some time, say a month, to negotiate and finalize the order with the vendor. Now if the offer validity of the vendor was till March 31st, then the same problem crops up as cited above.  
  2107→   
  2108→
  2109→However, there is an important point to note. Sometimes a client allows variable prices of items with respect to time based on a given formula. If such formulas really take care of the cost escalation over time, then there is nothing to worry. But if the bid was quoted with firm prices and the bid is extended based on client’s requirement, then there is a cause of worry in case the cost escalations are beyond the fixed prices quoted. In such a case, asking for more validity from vendors makes sense.  
  2110→Another important point to note is that after the bid is submitted and the bid is L1, and thereafter the client has procedural delays and intends to award beyond the validity period, the client will ask the bidder to extend his bid validity. In such a case, the bidder has every right not to extend his bid if it finds that it cannot sustain the cost escalations taken place over this period of time until it is compensated by the price variation formula of the client. 
  2111→
  2112→The irony is if the bidder does not wish to extend the validity of its bid after the bid is submitted which it has every legal right to do so, the client is often angry and tries to convince the bidder to extend its bid. This is because the client knows you have given the best price and therefore needs your extension of validity of the bid. Also usually, the client requires three valid techno-commercial responsive bids in order to award a bidder. 
  2113→
  2114→**The salient points of concern are:**
  2115→
  2116→1. Ask for one more month of validity from the vendors and subcontractors than that required in the bid so that SCM gets one month to negotiate and place orders in case the client awards the project at the fag end of the validity period of the bid.   
  2117→   2. Ensure that the vendors and contractors do not quote higher because you have asked for one month of validity of their offers.  
  2118→      3. As and when the client extends the bid validity before the bids are submitted, the bidder should ask similar extensions of offer validities from the vendors and the contractors.  
  2119→         4. In case of bid validity extensions before bid is submitted, bidders must evaluate cost escalations vis-a-vis the price variation formula the client has stipulated in the bid. In case of fixed price bids, bidders need to carefully evaluate based on extensions of validity given by their vendors.
  2120→
  2121→**CHAPTER : THE COMPLETION TIME ISSUE**
  2122→
  2123→In tender documents, the completion period required for executing a project is stipulated. The contractor may be executing many such similar projects at different sites. The Bid Manager may notice that the time taken to execute such projects or similar projects under execution is far more than the time stipulated in the current bid pertaining to a similar project.
  2124→
  2125→The Bid Manager consults with the Project Manager and understands that the Project Management costs in the bid must be considered for some more months than stipulated in the bid due to anticipated longer duration for completion. However, this will result in higher cost and therefore the bid may become uncompetitive. Even with the stipulated period of completion, the Bid Manager could consider “Crashing” of the project, in which case again the bid becomes uncompetitive.
  2126→
  2127→Sometimes in such cases there arises a conflict between the Bid Manager and the Project Manager. The Bid Manager, to become competitive, intends to consider the stipulated completion period for the Project Management Costs asking the Project team to become more efficient henceforth, whereas the Project Manager wants that the Bid Manager consider some more months for project completion because he anticipates a longer duration for project execution. And sometimes they quarrel and fight\!\!\!
  2128→
  2129→The above scenario happens in many organisations. The basic problem here is that the Project team thinks that winning bids is not his responsibility and his KRA, promotions and increment shall be irrespective of bids won or lost by the Bidding team. Therefore, in one of my chapters, I have raised a serious issue; is only the Bidding team responsible for winning or losing bids? Or, equally responsible are the Designs cell, the Project cell, the Finance cell who provides key data affecting the overall bid cost?
  2130→
  2131→The Project team also fails to note one very important aspect of business. If a Bidding team does not win bids, there will be no projects to be executed by the Project team. An organization generates turnover and profits and thereby provides salaries only when projects are executed. Without project execution, none gets salary including the Project team. And without bidding competitively, the organization does not win projects. It is therefore in the interest of all employees of all departments and the organization that everyone support the Bidding team to be competitive. To do so, all departments must find means to be more efficient. Project Team may also need to find means to be more effective and complete projects in stipulated period of time so that the bidding team does not unnecessarily consider higher Project management costs and become uncompetitive.
  2132→
  2133→In today’s world, cost of inefficiency cannot be considered in bid. If you are inefficient and escalate costs because of your efficiency, you will cease to be a player in the market. Either you be efficient or perish in the market. Let not a bid become uncompetitive because of inefficiency of Project team or any other department who contribute significantly to bid costs.
  2134→
  2135→**The salient points of concern are:**
  2136→
  2137→1. Since bidding team have to put their most competitive bid, Project team should not insist Bidding team to consider more months of project execution than stipulated in the bid until and unless it is really practically not feasible.  
  2138→   2. Project team and all other departments must find efficient means to provide their most competitive costs to the bidding team for them to consider in their bids.  
  2139→      3. In today’s competitive environment, cost of inefficiency cannot be considered in a bid. If you do so, you will cease to be in the industry.
  2140→
  2141→  
  2142→
  2143→**CHAPTER : THE ONLY CULPRIT FOR LOSING BIDS**
  2144→
  2145→The Marketing or the Bidding Department of an organisation is reprimanded for failure to secure orders. Yes, they are the final decider of the price bids submitted by them, hence reprimanded for losses. But the story should not end here.
  2146→
  2147→There are several costs built up in a bid. Manpower costs are primarily driven by HR. The more optimised, effective and experienced manpower are recruited by HR, the more the bid cost will be optimised. Supply Chain (SC) Costs is another major cost built up in a bid. The SCM Department is responsible for evaluating and recommending the best responsive yet cost competitive vendors in the bid. Failing to do so will jack up bid prices. Finance is another significant contributor to bid cost. Finance Department must have the best negotiated fianance costs with partner banks so that the cash flow finance costs built up in a bid are as less as possible. The overhead costs of the organisation must be reasonable. Often it is found that the top heavy management increases the overheads and therefore the bid cost. Logistics cost provided by the Fleet Department should also be optimised. The right technology and equipment to be used during the project execution as determined by the Technology Department also has a reasonable share in the bid cost. And very importantly, it is the Designs Department who provide design data based on which product and service costs are determined and considered in a bid. Bad designs alone can render a bid highly uncompetitive in spite of other factors being optimised. The Marketing team too provides the proportionate cost of marketing and advertising in each bid.
  2148→
  2149→Therefore, I find it amusing that during appraisals, the Bidding Department is either facilitated or punished alone for securing or not being able to secure orders. The Bidding team actually compiles costs obtained from various departments and forms a bid just like a music director composes the singing with other instruments to form a song. However, since the bidding team is responsible to have a market intelligence of probable winning levels, its role to negotiate and accept costs from all other departments cannot be under estimated. At the same time, other departments who provide and contribute to bid costs cannot be ignored and be scot free for bid losses.

4500→Also, don’t miss your annual or bi-annual basic health check-ups comprising of blood tests, Chest x-ray, USG and ECG. It is interesting to note that many people think they are fine and healthy but health check-ups reveal that certain health parameters are beyond normal values which require immediate treatment. What I am trying to sensitise that many such illness develop within our bodies SILENTLY.  For example, thousands of people do not know that they are pre-diabetic or have high blood pressure because they don’t check. These are silent killers. So please don’t miss your health check-ups.    
  4501→**Health is wealth. It is one of the most precious assets that one possesses. Please don’t ignore**  
  4502→   
  4503→**PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT**
  4504→
  4505→This is again a ignored subject while we are in service. Someone comes and convinces of investing in a FD, we do it. Someone comes and asks to invest in ULIPs, we do it. Someone else comes and convinces us that there is lot of money to be earned in the stock market, we invest in the equities. Someone says to buy a flat instead of paying rents, we do so. That someone could be you yourself. You may think to put some money in gold, you do it. Whatever someone tries to convince or we are convinced about investment, we do it. However, all these investments are done in a haphazard manner.
  4506→
  4507→Here comes the issue of portfolio management. There is no harm in investing money in different class of assets, but it has to be done in a scientific manner. Firstly, the class of assets can be classified into equity, debt, property and metal. The percentage of investment into these different class could be based on one's age (the less the age, the more may be invested in equities), risk taking power (the less the risk taking power is, the more may be invested in debts), interest (if on a transferable job, someone may not be interested in property). 
  4508→
  4509→A typical mid aged person with moderate risk taking power can invest like;  
  4510→35% on equity  
  4511→25% on debt  
  4512→25% on property  
  4513→15% on metal
  4514→
  4515→Equity could be the stock market or equity MFs. Debt could be bank FD, corporate FD and corporate bonds. Property could be residential or commercial. Metal could be physical gold or silver, paper gold or silver, platinum or copper, zinc and aluminum in the MCX stock exchange. 
  4516→
  4517→Please take the help of a portfolio manager, who could be your Finance guy himself, to guide you for a good portfolio.
  4518→
  4519→**Invest wisely to maintain a good portfolio which will lead to earning good profits**
  4520→
  4521→**WORK-LIFE BALANCE**
  4522→
  4523→This is a hot debate often happening even on TV debates. How many live s have God given you? One, I suppose. Therefore you are never going to get the time lapsed of your life. Even the richest man on earth cannot buy time which has lapsed. Time gone is gone forever. And your past is also gone forever.
  4524→
  4525→So, while we have to struggle very hard in the corporates to do well in life and rise up the corporate ladder, we also need to give time for yourself (me time) and your family and friends. All is not work in life. We definitely have to work; it is a foundation to have a great life with our family and friends. So you need to balance between work and life and that is what is called the Work-Life balance. If you provide all the time to work, your relationship with your family may not be so intimate and loving, you may lose your friends and you may end up leaving all your hobbies that you so much loved to pursue.
  4526→
  4527→For a few days the balance may be upset due to pressure of work or on family matters, but on an overall basis you must strike a balance between work and life.
  4528→
  4529→**WORK-LIFE balance is about providing time to office as well as to our near and dear ones.**
  4530→
  4531→**GRATEFULNESS**
  4532→
  4533→Why do we touch the feet of our parents when we meet them? Why do we touch the feet of our beloved school teachers or our coaches? Because we are grateful to them for the care, love and teachings they have imparted to us. Without their care and support, we would not have been what we are; therefore they have been large contributors to our success in life.
  4534→
  4535→However, very unfortunately, I have seen many people not being grateful to their contributors once the contributor is no more in a position to contribute to you anymore. For example, as a business Head, you might have taught the intricacies of business to your subordinate and encouraged him and promoted him to assume higher responsibilities which he was capable of, but as you retire and you are no more in a position to give good bonus or promote him any further you are simply forgotten. It is often said that in a street or a market a clerk or a staff do not even look at a Director who have retired from his organization because the clerk knows that the Director now cannot give him any benefits further. These people are so selfish because they think why they should waste time, energy and money to talk or meet their bosses when they are useless for them\!  
  4536→
  4537→Above kind of incidences happen in every sphere of life, even within our relatives and “so called” friends. So why should someone be grateful. It has been proven scientifically they gratefulness leads to:
  4538→
  4539→1) Emotional well-being: Gratitude fosters positive emotions, reduces stress. Expressing appreciation leads to increased happiness and satisfaction of the soul  
  4540→2) Physical well-being: Gratitude improves physical health by promoting better sleep, reduces blood pressure and enhances immunity  
  4541→3) Mental well-being: Gratitude lowers depression and anxiety and helps in facing challenges in life  
  4542→4) Social connections: Gratitude strengthens relationships and reduces loneliness  
  4543→5) Contentment: Gratitude leads to greater contentment. It shifts focus from what one lacks to what one has promoting a sense of fulfillment
  4544→
  4545→**Practice gratefulness; you may not be aware how it improves your well-being**
  4546→
  4547→**WEIGH YOUR OPTIONS**
  4548→
  4549→I find it frustrating when one tries to figure out only the negative sides of a person, an option or an entity. Be it yourself, or a singer, or an actor, or a politician, or a team, or a political party, or a school, or whatever, there can be good things as well as bad things about it. Any human or agency or entity cannot be perfect in all parameters. But a good human or an agency or an entity has presumably many more good things than the bad things about them. You need to appreciate those good things and not ignore and focus on bad things. You can also weigh the importance of the good things and the bad things and come to a conclusion if the entity is good or bad.
  4550→
  4551→In our personal life and also in the corporate, we sometimes have many options to select from. You must weigh the positive and negative factors of each of the options and come to a conclusion on which option to choose. It is obvious that all options will have some negative points. There are people who are not happy with any option because he tries to see only the negative side of each option. These people cannot make proper and fast decisions needed in the corporate world. You have to choose an option with the greatest advantages amidst some disadvantages that it may have. 
  4552→
  4553→**SABJANTALOGY**
  4554→
  4555→This is the name of a subject which I coined after coming across some people who fit into this class of people. Just like Biology, Physiology, Oncology, Sociology, Anthropology, etc., Sabjantalogy deals with the science of knowing everything on earth.
  4556→
  4557→Some people may advise you on how to cook better, advise you on better medicines, teach you how to play a sports better, seemingly knows about any kind of industry on earth, knows about fabric and garments, gives you lecture on politics, or even on rocket science. The most astonishing part is that all these “knowhow??” has been formed on his own whims and fancies and not formed by reading books or learning from somewhere.
  4558→
  4559→Say for example, if you are an Arts graduate and have formed an idea about a medicine with your own experiment without consulting a doctor or learning it from any medical journal or reading about it from newspaper or elsewhere, is it right for you to prescribe the medicine to your friend? There can be hundreds of examples like this on various subjects.
  4560→
  4561→Today the world has lot of expertise in every field of life. If you really want to prescribe a medicine, ask the doctor, or if you really want to advise on how to make the best cakes, ten consult youtube or a chef. Why provide your “so called” knowledge which is actually wrong.
  4562→
  4563→**Don’t be a Sabtanjalogist; ask the specialist or learn about it.**  
  4564→
  4565→**DON’T BE A MISER**
  4566→
  4567→What is the purpose of earning money; to spend it wisely and reasonably; Isn’t it? But some accumulate money for their own satisfaction and not spend them for reasons not known to them. They are misers. Misers are of two types: universal misers and selfish misers. Universal misers are those who are reluctant to spend for anyone on earth. Selfish misers are those who are willing to spend only for him and family but not beyond.
  4568→
  4569→Spending must be wise and reasonable; you need to keep reasonable amount of money for medical emergencies, education and marriage of your children and for all mandatory purposes of life. Beyond that, you should spend for yourself, family and others for things that you love in life, be it for food, movies, clothes, gadget, tours and travels, hobbies or whatever that you think will be useful or make someone happy. Remember, there is no concept of Fixed Deposits in heaven. Everything that you earn in life has to be left. We have been born naked and will depart to Heaven naked. It is one life that God has given us. Enjoy it to the fullest extent. 
  4570→
  4571→How much to save, how much to invest and how much to spend may vary between individuals, but they have to be wisely and reasonably thought of practically. Allot money that you earn for savings, investment and expenses in a balanced practical manner.
  4572→
  4573→**Save & invest what is necessary. But spend too to enjoy and live your life.** 
  4574→
  4575→**SPEND TIME WITH YOUR CHILD**
  4576→
  4577→There is a joke which you will hear while your child grows up. When you are asked how tall your child has grown up, you answer by gesture with your hands apart horizontally. This is because your child leaves for school early while you are asleep and the child is asleep when you come home late from office. Therefore you have not seen him grow tall vertically on office days. Suddenly you can see him how tall he is when you are at home on Sundays. 
  4578→
  4579→However, do not take it as a simple joke. Once your child is 18 years old and pass out Class XII, he may be going out of station or abroad for higher studies and subsequently for good jobs anywhere on this earth. This means you may get to stay with your child at your home only for the first 18 years of his life and thereafter you will miss him except for a few days when the child will have time to meet you at home on his holidays.
  4580→
  4581→At the same time, you will be very busy at office trying to secure your good future year after year. But amidst this rat race, do not ignore to spend as much time as possible with your child. Remember you may not get to see him for long after he reaches 18 years of age. Your good times spent with your child will remain etched in your mind forever. Do not miss these times. It will never come back again 
  4582→
  4583→**CULTIVATE A HOBBY**
  4584→
  4585→There will be times in your life when you are very sad. Maybe your boss did not give you a promotion which you thought of sure to get, or could be your fiancé decided for a break up, or incidents like these. You would not like to be besides of your friends or family at such sad moments. You would prefer to be alone for some while. So how do you spend these times alone? Cultivate a hobby which will keep you happy and busy to tide over your sad times or after your retirement. You can learn to play cards on your own; you can learn to play an instrument; you can learn to sing; you can learn to paint; or any other hobby that will give you a sense of fulfillment.
  4586→
  4587→Music was my greatest de-stressor. Playing the harmonica and the guitar lit up my soul at sad times. These instruments are still a part of my life after retirement. 
  4588→
  4589→**DEMONSTRATE UNBIASED PROFESIONALISM**
  4590→
  4591→I have read it everywhere that even a peon would not like to wish or see your face on the street after you have retired as a Director or from a big position in the corporate. This may be true but not always. 
  4592→
  4593→As a leader, if you had shown good leadership by demonstrating correct attitude, being kind and supportive, managing people humanly with care and respect, being grateful to people who supported you, encouraging people to do well and rewarding them for good work and your actions on employees and all stakeholders always being unbiased and transparent and to top it all if you had been successful in your organization, then people, whoever knew you and know you presently hearing from others do remember and respect you even after you retire or even die. 
  4594→
  4595→Being unbiased even with your close colleagues and demonstrating your professionalism against personal bias at all times is seen as a great leadership. Such successful leaders are never forgotten.  
  4596→
  4597→**ENJOY LIFE**
  4598→
  4599→The Almighty have given you ONE life only. Even if your super rich, you have one life only. So always enjoy your life.
  4600→
  4601→How many hours do you have in a year? 24 hours a day x365 days a year \= 8760 hours. Assume you sleep 8 hours a day; therefore you spend 8 x 365 hours \= 2920 hours. Assume you work 10 hours a day (except Sundays and 12 annual holidays) and commute 2 hours a day to office; therefore you spend 12 x (365-52-12) hours \= 3612 hours. So you have (8760-2920-3612) hours, ie. 2228 hours a year excluding time for work and sleep. 2228 hours out of 8760 hours means around 25% of your life; could be less if you work more. Have you ever imagined that you have only 25% of your life free of work and sleep which you can utilize for yourself, family and friends and relatives? If you consider 5% time gone for maintaining your house, household works, taking your child to school and tuitions, purchase of daily groceries, etc. then you are left with 20% of your life time only. Sounds pretty less\! But that is the fact of life. So enjoy your life well with this 20% of your time. It shall also rejuvenate you to carry on with your work at office. You can do so by allotting time for:
  4602→
  4603→1) Savouring good food at home and restaurants  
  4604→2) Entertainment like hobbies, movies, fairs, gossiping with friends, etc  
  4605→3) Tours and Travels – local, out of station and international destinations
  4606→
  4607→Sorry, if I have missed anything that you would like to enjoy life it. Whatever it is, remember what Mahatma Gandhi said \- “Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever”
  4608→
  4609→**SEEING THROUGH DISRUPTORS**
  4610→
  4611→Today you will come across many disrupters during your personal and corporate life. 
  4612→
  4613→**Technological disruptors** will force you to change methods and techniques in doing your work and execute your project. As a simple example, when computers came in, typists were forced to learn software like Word, Excel, etc. to type on computers since the type writers were abolished. Any typist who did not do so lost his job.
  4614→
  4615→**Geo-political disruptors** will force you to change the way you do business. As an example the tariff episodes between USA and many other countries including India is forcing many countries to look into other avenues for their business. Such disruptors play havoc on the stock markets with their volatility.
  4616→
  4617→**Social disruptors** like break up of marriage, divorce, suicide, crimes, etc. will disturb your peace of mind which can affect your personal and corporate life. 
  4618→
  4619→**Environmental disruptors** like pollution, food adulteration, climate change, etc. is taking a toll in our health.
  4620→
  4621→In the spate of all these disruptors, you have to see through them with calm and pass them off because most are external inflicted where you have no control. It is easier said than done, but better if it can be done. You need to build a strong mind which is not easy to be disrupted. You must develop capabilities to control your mind and not allow your mind to take control of you.  
  4622→
  4623→**QUIT IF UNHAPPY**
  4624→
  4625→You can work in your company in two ways; one is with stress or pressure that is on you to perform and the other is with passion. 
  4626→
  4627→When you work with passion, you forget how long you have been working on because you work with interest not by any external force or pressure to perform. When you work for long with stress and pressure, you will undergo hormonal changes in your body harming your health. Try to find a new suitable job where you can work happily before your health deteriorates. I know finding a new job is not easy, but go on trying to save your health. Quit.  
  4628→
  4629→**CONTENTMENT IN LIFE**
  4630→
  4631→Once a middle aged officer approached me in my cabin and informed that he would like to resign because of his low pay. I was astonished to hear about the low pay and called him along with the HR manager later on. On scrutiny of his pay slip, I found that his take home pay was very less but also observed that there were lot of deductions from his salary. On deeper scrutiny I found that there were several EMIs being deducted from his salary, one for new 3BHK property, one for SUV car, one for a racing bike and another for a 75 inch LED TV. I had to only convey that the company is providing you a reasonable salary but it is due to your heavy purchases that your take home salary is meager. 
  4632→
  4633→Today many youngsters wants to have a property, car and a bike within latest 30 years of age, forget about other costly gadgets which they term them as necessary. There is no end to a materialistic world today. You must put a full stop to buying at some point of time. If you have a hatchback, you will want to have a sedan, then a SUV, then a Mercedes or a BMW, then a Ferrari, where is the end? There is no contentment in your life them, and therefore you will always remain unhappy. You need to balance your pocket. As I said earlier, you have to allot money wisely and reasonably for your savings, investment and your expenses, the total being what comes to your pocket.
  4634→
  4635→If you develop the Attitude to use money judicially and be happy with whatever you have, you will achieve a state of contentment. Contentment is about being satisfied and happy with whatever you have in life. Contentment does not look at what you do not have in life. **Achieve a state of contentment; you will be happy in life**
  4636→
  4637→**PART 30: SOME LESSONS I LEARNT**
  4638→
  4639→**The Tongue**  
  4640→What is the most important organ of human life? Obviously the heart which pumps blood more than 100,000 times a day, which means if you live for 75 years, it will pump approx. 2.75 billion times. Most miraculously, it pumps on its own; you don’t do anything for it to pump, except for taking care of it sometimes with good food and aerobic exercise. With the blood being pumped by the heart, all other organs play their vital role in their own domains.
  4641→
  4642→But what is the most important organ for living a good life? I say “the tongue”. Tongue is the only organ for the sense of taste and unhealthy but tasty food destroys our health. Food is a vital factor of our health other than some other lifestyle habits. So our tongue is responsible for our physical health. 
  4643→
  4644→Also we can speak because of our tongue. The way we speak determines our character and this character determines your relationship, be it with your family or friends or relatives or neighbours or colleagues in the corporate. The way politicians speak also determines his relationship with the public because politicians are public figures. So tongue is the main organ for your relationships. It is said that deaf and dumb do not have enemies; yes, they may have the tongue but cannot speak. 
  4645→
  4646→**Lesson**: The tongue is responsible for two major aspects in one’s life; your physical health and your relationships. Watch your tongue from now on.  
  4647→\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
  4648→
  4649→**True Friends**

content.text: Let me continue reading the end of the file:

content.content:
4700→**Lesson**: Appreciate performers; Encourage and Reward them  
  4701→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4702→
  4703→**Silence**  
  4704→Sometimes when we argue and protest for something, the other person may be adamant, not willing to listen to your logic, and keep on arguing to prove that you are wrong. It happens in the corporate too. Particularly when you argue with your seniors like a senior relative or your boss to put your points in place and the other person does not agree and keep on arguing, you find yourself frustrated wasting unnecessary time. In such times, I have stayed away from such people with no contacts at all for long durations. In the corporate, you can maintain silence to the extent possible except for when you need to discuss or interact. Often I have observed that your silence speaks much louder than words and helps the other person to realize that he was adamant and wrong. I have adopted this policy even with my relatives whenever required.
  4705→
  4706→**Lesson**: Silence is often the best form of protest  
  4707→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4708→
  4709→**Be Respectful**  
  4710→Once my mother rebuked me for no wrong I had done. I gave her back. I did not understand the pain I inflicted on her. I loved her very much and I repented that I was harsh and aggressive. I could have replied in a much respectful manner and not insult her, however, sticking to my point of view.
  4711→
  4712→In a similar manner, I had scolded a colleague very harshly who was much junior in position to me but much senior to me by age. I saw him almost on the verge of collapsing. I learnt one big lesson in life. Even if you have to reprimand someone, particularly higher in age, for something, you can do it in a respectful manner without being harsh. If required, both can share their point of view for better understanding. Even strict punishments can be given as and when necessary, but it has to be in a respectful manner and humanly because older people probably cannot digest insult as easily as they could when they were young. Be polite and respectful to senior people.  That is a sign of good leadership.
  4713→
  4714→**Lesson**: Be respectful; It is a sign of good leadership  
  4715→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4716→
  4717→**Whatever happens, it happens for good**  
  4718→Look at so many incidences I narrated before. I met with an accident while coming home from a seminar, but the incident revealed who my true friends were. I was hospitalized after a severe chest pain and underwent angioplasty, but the event taught me to live healthier and realize that Health is Wealth. I did not get promotion for long eight years in my early part of my career, but it helped me to be more perseverant and live with Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence. When great leaders like the former Tata group Chairman and my MD facilitated me for my success, I learnt what appreciation by humble and caring leaders mean. When seniors accused me wrongly, I learnt how silence can be the best form of protest.
  4719→
  4720→Even when my father and mother died, I thought that it is for their good that their souls would be relieved from the sufferings and pain they were in. It pained me a lot on their demise, but I believed that the truth of death being certain is a creation of the Almighty for something good. Maybe that helped me to become independent from the shelter of my parents and be able to do tough work for which I was dependent on my parents. Loss of your loved one probably provides you greater strength to live on with your life, though at times the emotions of the loss is immeasurable. 
  4721→
  4722→In every undesired event, I learnt something new. Maybe that is the positive way to look at undesirable events. I am not here to prove that every undesirable event will lead to learning something new. But believing in “whatever happens, it happens for good” provides strength to live on with hope of something good lies ahead, triggers new thought process and new learning and helps to tide over such bad or sad uneventful times.

content.content:
\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Appreciate Performers**  
Once I started doing well as the Marketing Head winning sizeable number of bids, my MD wished that the top 10 performers of the company, which included me too, be facilitated by the then Chairman of the Tata Group, Mr Cyrus Mistry. It was a moment in my corporate life to be cherished forever. Mr Mistry came to our HO at Hyderabad and facilitated me and the others with a sincere word of appreciation. I cannot forget ever the gesture of my MD and the Chairman to have done this for me. I remain ever grateful to them. And later in life, I cried when I heard the news of Mr Mistry’s death.

In another instance, when I was already succeeding as Marketing Head winning bids, my MD came to our office for a meeting with my bosses. The bosses accompanied the MD upstairs to our office and headed for his cabin past my own. When he saw me, he first came inside my cabin along with my bosses and almost fell on my feet.  I was shocked. I said “No No sir, please do not do that”. He said “Abhik, winning bids only allow our company to execute projects and everyone has a job to do in this company only when you win bids, so I am grateful to you and your team”. Tears rolled down my years on seeing his appreciation. I had no words to say.

**Lesson**: Appreciate performers; Encourage and Reward them  
\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Silence**  
Sometimes when we argue and protest for something, the other person may be adamant, not willing to listen to your logic, and keep on arguing to prove that you are wrong. It happens in the corporate too. Particularly when you argue with your seniors like a senior relative or your boss to put your points in place and the other person does not agree and keep on arguing, you find yourself frustrated wasting unnecessary time. In such times, I have stayed away from such people with no contacts at all for long durations. In the corporate, you can maintain silence to the extent possible except for when you need to discuss or interact. Often I have observed that your silence speaks much louder than words and helps the other person to realize that he was adamant and wrong. I have adopted this policy even with my relatives whenever required.

**Lesson**: Silence is often the best form of protest  
\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Be Respectful**  
Once my mother rebuked me for no wrong I had done. I gave her back. I did not understand the pain I inflicted on her. I loved her very much and I repented that I was harsh and aggressive. I could have replied in a much respectful manner and not insult her, however, sticking to my point of view.

In a similar manner, I had scolded a colleague very harshly who was much junior in position to me but much senior to me by age. I saw him almost on the verge of collapsing. I learnt one big lesson in life. Even if you have to reprimand someone, particularly higher in age, for something, you can do it in a respectful manner without being harsh. If required, both can share their point of view for better understanding. Even strict punishments can be given as and when necessary, but it has to be in a respectful manner and humanly because older people probably cannot digest insult as easily as they could when they were young. Be polite and respectful to senior people.  That is a sign of good leadership.

**Lesson**: Be respectful; It is a sign of good leadership  
\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Whatever happens, it happens for good**  
Look at so many incidences I narrated before. I met with an accident while coming home from a seminar, but the incident revealed who my true friends were. I was hospitalized after a severe chest pain and underwent angioplasty, but the event taught me to live healthier and realize that Health is Wealth. I did not get promotion for long eight years in my early part of my career, but it helped me to be more perseverant and live with Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence. When great leaders like the former Tata group Chairman and my MD facilitated me for my success, I learnt what appreciation by humble and caring leaders mean. When seniors accused me wrongly, I learnt how silence can be the best form of protest.

Even when my father and mother died, I thought that it is for their good that their souls would be relieved from the sufferings and pain they were in. It pained me a lot on their demise, but I believed that the truth of death being certain is a creation of the Almighty for something good. Maybe that helped me to become independent from the shelter of my parents and be able to do tough work for which I was dependent on my parents. Loss of your loved one probably provides you greater strength to live on with your life, though at times the emotions of the loss is immeasurable. 

In every undesired event, I learnt something new. Maybe that is the positive way to look at undesirable events. I am not here to prove that every undesirable event will lead to learning something new. But believing in “whatever happens, it happens for good” provides strength to live on with hope of something good lies ahead, triggers new thought process and new learning and helps to tide over such bad or sad uneventful times.
